# Pyreon Framework

> Signal-based UI framework for TypeScript. SSR, SSG, islands, SPA. Standard JSX + TypeScript.
> Top-tier performance on the synthetic row-list benchmark — fastest of the frameworks measured, leads or ties Solid on 8 of 9 ops (Solid edges single-row remove), and 2.4–3× faster than React/Svelte/Preact at 10k rows. Mid-pack on retained memory.

## Signals (reactivity)

```typescript
import { signal, computed, effect, batch } from "@pyreon/reactivity"

const count = signal(0)       // create
count()                        // read (subscribes)
count.set(5)                   // write
count.update(n => n + 1)       // update
count.peek()                   // read without subscribing

const doubled = computed(() => count() * 2)  // auto-tracks deps
effect(() => console.log(count()))           // runs when count changes
batch(() => { a.set(1); b.set(2) })          // single notification
```

## Components

```typescript
// Components are plain functions that run ONCE
const Counter = (props: { initial: number }) => {
  const count = signal(props.initial)
  return <button onClick={() => count.update(n => n + 1)}>{count()}</button>
}
```

## Conditional Rendering

```typescript
import { Show, Switch, Match } from "@pyreon/core"

<Show when={isLoggedIn()} fallback={<Login />}>
  <Dashboard />
</Show>
```

## List Rendering

```typescript
import { For } from "@pyreon/core"

// Use "by" not "key" (JSX reserves "key" for VNode reconciliation)
<For each={items()} by={item => item.id}>
  {item => <li>{item.name}</li>}
</For>
```

## Lifecycle

```typescript
import { onMount, onUnmount, provide } from "@pyreon/core"
import { onCleanup } from "@pyreon/reactivity"

onMount(() => {
  const id = setInterval(tick, 1000)
  return () => clearInterval(id)  // optional cleanup
})
```

## Context

```typescript
import { createContext, useContext, provide } from "@pyreon/core"

const Theme = createContext<"light" | "dark">("light")

// Provide (auto-cleans up on unmount):
provide(Theme, "dark")

// Consume:
const theme = useContext(Theme)
```

## Smart `class` with `cx()`

```typescript
import { cx } from "@pyreon/core"

// Strings, arrays, objects, nested mix — all work:
<div class="base active" />
<div class={["base", isActive() && "active"]} />
<div class={{ base: true, active: isActive() }} />
<div class={cx("base", isActive() && "active", { bold: isBold() })} />
```

## Props Utilities

```typescript
import { splitProps, mergeProps, createUniqueId } from "@pyreon/core"

// Split props preserving reactivity:
const [local, rest] = splitProps(props, ["class", "style"])

// Merge default props:
const merged = mergeProps({ size: "md", variant: "primary" }, props)

// SSR-safe unique IDs:
const id = createUniqueId()  // "pyreon-1", "pyreon-2", etc.
```

## Typed Events

```typescript
// TargetedEvent types currentTarget per element — no casts needed:
<input onInput={(e: TargetedEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
  console.log(e.currentTarget.value)  // typed!
}} />
```

## Effect Cleanup

```typescript
import { effect, onCleanup } from "@pyreon/reactivity"

effect(() => {
  const handler = () => console.log(count())
  window.addEventListener("resize", handler)
  onCleanup(() => window.removeEventListener("resize", handler))
})
```

## Routing

```typescript
import { createRouter, RouterProvider, RouterView, RouterLink, useRouter, useRoute, useTypedSearchParams, useTransition, useMiddlewareData } from "@pyreon/router"

const router = createRouter([
  { path: "/", component: Home },
  { path: "/user/:id", component: User, loader: ({ params }) => fetchUser(params.id) },
])

<RouterProvider router={router}>
  <RouterLink to="/">Home</RouterLink>
  <RouterView />
</RouterProvider>

// Navigation:
const router = useRouter()
router.push("/settings")
router.back()
```

## Head Management

```typescript
import { useHead } from "@pyreon/head"

useHead({ title: "Page Title", meta: [{ name: "description", content: "..." }] })
useHead(() => ({ title: `${name()} — Profile` }))  // reactive
```

## SSR

```typescript
import { createHandler } from "@pyreon/server"

export default createHandler({ App, routes, mode: "stream" })
```

## Islands (Partial Hydration)

```typescript
import { island } from "@pyreon/server"

const SearchBar = island(() => import("./SearchBar"), { name: "SearchBar", hydrate: "visible" })
```

## Mount (Client)

```typescript
import { mount } from "@pyreon/runtime-dom"

mount(<App />, document.getElementById("app")!)
```

## Common Mistakes — NEVER DO THIS

### Signals
- `{count}` → `{count()}` — signals are functions, call them to read
- `signal(5)` → `signal.set(5)` — calling signal with args does NOT write (dev mode warns)
- `.value` → signals are callable, not `.value` getters (that's Vue)
- `useState` → `signal()`, `useEffect` → `effect()` or `onMount()`
- `memo(Component)` → not needed, components run once in Pyreon

### JSX
- `key={...}` on `<For>` → use `by={...}` prop (`key` is reserved for VNode reconciliation)
- `className` → `class`, `htmlFor` → `for` (standard HTML attributes)
- `onChange` on `<input>` → `onInput` for keypress-by-keypress (native DOM events)
- `.map()` in JSX → use `<For>` for reactive list rendering
- `class="a b"` works, but for conditional classes use `class={["a", cond && "b"]}` or `cx()`

### Props & Reactivity
- `const { state } = props` → BREAKS REACTIVITY! Destructuring captures once. Use `props.state` for reactive access
- `const x = props.y` in JSX is NOW REACTIVE — compiler inlines `props.y` at JSX use sites (text + attributes). Transitive: `const a = props.x; const b = a + 1` inlines as `((props.x) + 1)`. Only `const` tracked (`let`/`var` excluded). Non-JSX usage stays static.
- For rest props with reactivity: `const [own, rest] = splitProps(props, ['state', 'size'])` — preserves getters
- Component props with signal reads ARE reactive (compiler wraps with _rp): `<Button state={isActive() ? 'primary' : 'default'} />` updates without remount
- Single JSX element props are stable: `<Wrapper icon={<Icon name={x()} />} />` — Icon created once, name updates reactively
- Conditional JSX props remount: `<Wrapper icon={show() ? <Icon /> : null} />` — correct, different VNode types

### Context & Effects
- `const { mode } = useContext(ctx)` → BREAKS REACTIVITY! Destructuring captures once. Keep reference: `const ctx = useContext(Ctx)` then `ctx.mode`
- `effect(() => { effect(() => ...) })` → NEVER nest effects. Use `computed()` for derived values
- `.peek()` inside effects/computeds → bypasses tracking, creates stale reads

### Providers
- Use `<PyreonUI theme={theme} mode="light">` — this is the ONLY provider you need
- Do NOT use `UnistyleProvider`, `ThemeProvider`, or `CoreProvider` directly — they are internal

### Other
- `(e.target as HTMLInputElement)` → use `TargetedEvent<HTMLInputElement>` for typed `currentTarget`
- `onMount(() => { doStuff() })` → can optionally return a cleanup function
- `onClick={maybeUndefined}` → safe (runtime bails), but be aware the handler is checked at assignment time

## Core Framework (10 packages)

- @pyreon/reactivity — Fine-grained reactivity: signal, computed, effect, batch, onCleanup, createStore, watch, createResource, untrack. Pyreon signals are NOT `.value` getters (Vue ref) or `[state, setState]` tuples (React useState). The signal IS the function: `count()` reads, `count.set(v)` writes, `count.update(fn)` derives. This is the #1 confusion for developers coming from other frameworks.
- @pyreon/core — VNode, h(), Fragment, lifecycle, context, JSX runtime, Suspense, ErrorBoundary, lazy(), Dynamic, cx(), splitProps, mergeProps, createUniqueId. Pyreon components are plain functions that execute a single time. Reactivity comes from reading signals inside reactive scopes (JSX expression thunks, `effect()`, `computed()`), not from re-running the component function. `if (!cond()) return null` at the top level runs once and is static — use `return (() => { if (!cond()) return null; return <div /> })` for reactive conditional rendering.
- @pyreon/compiler — JSX reactive transform (Rust native + JS fallback) plus the Reactivity-Lens sidecar, React→Pyreon migration, and project audits. Reverting `src/jsx.ts` (the JS path) is INVISIBLE to anything that goes through the native binary — the Rust path in `native/src/lib.rs` is a parallel implementation, kept byte-identical by the cross-backend equivalence tests. Edits to transform behavior must land in BOTH; the equivalence suite is the gate.
- @pyreon/runtime-dom — DOM renderer, mount, hydrateRoot, Transition, TransitionGroup, KeepAlive, SVG/MathML namespace, custom elements. SVG and MathML elements ALWAYS use `setAttribute()` for prop forwarding, never property assignment. Many SVG properties (`markerWidth`, `refX`, etc.) are read-only `SVGAnimatedLength` getters — `el[key] = value` crashes. Detected by `el.namespaceURI !== "http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml"`.
- @pyreon/runtime-server — SSR/SSG VNode→HTML renderer — renderToString, renderToStream (out-of-order Suspense), per-request ALS context/store isolation. Signal accessors are called synchronously to snapshot their value — no effects are created. A `{count()}` expression renders the value at render time; it only becomes live after client hydration. SSR is one-shot.
- @pyreon/router — hash+history+SSR, context-based, prefetching, guards, loaders, useIsActive, View Transitions, middleware, typed search params. `await router.push()` resolves after `updateCallbackDone` (DOM commit), NOT after animation finishes. It does NOT wait for `.finished` (~200-300ms). `.ready` and `.finished` get empty `.catch()` handlers so `AbortError: Transition was skipped` rejections (from interrupted transitions) do not leak as unhandled promise rejections.
- @pyreon/head — Reactive `<head>` tag management — useHead(), HeadProvider, renderWithHead() for SSR. Tags with the same key replace each other (innermost wins). Meta keys: `name` → `property` → index. Link keys: `href + rel` → `rel` → index. Script keys: `src` → index. Style and noscript are unkeyed and always accumulated.
- @pyreon/server — SSR + SSG + island architecture — createHandler(), prerender(), island(), serverIsland(), middleware chain. `mode: "stream"` uses `renderToStream` with shell + app + tail emission; Suspense boundaries resolve out-of-order. The `<head>` collected via `@pyreon/head` is flushed in the shell before any Suspense boundaries resolve, so async-loaded data does not contribute to it.
- @pyreon/primitives — 15 cross-platform UI primitives that compile to DOM + SwiftUI + Compose from one .tsx: Stack, Inline, Layer, Scroll, Spacer (layout); Text, Heading, Image, Icon (content); Button, Press, Link (interaction); Field, Toggle, Modal (input). Plus <WebView> (host a web-only component natively, bidirectional bridge) and <Web>/<NativeIOS>/<NativeAndroid> escape hatches.
- @pyreon/sized-map — Bounded Map<K, V> — evicts the oldest entry at a maxEntries cap; FIFO (default) or LRU-on-read mode. FIFO fits hot paths where a per-read recency bump (a `delete` + `set` pair) would dominate the real work (runtime-dom `_tplCache`, router loader/component caches). LRU-on-read fits caches where frequently-read entries must survive small caps (rocketstyle `_rsMemo`, `@pyreon/lint` AstCache, `@pyreon/zero` ISR `createMemoryStore`).

## UI System (18 packages)

- @pyreon/ui-core — Unified `PyreonUI` provider (theme + mode + config), `useMode()` hook, init() escape hatch. The legacy split (separate theme / mode / config providers) is removed. `PyreonUI` is the only correct mount; calling `init()` directly is the escape hatch for SSR or test environments where the provider tree is unavailable.
- @pyreon/styler — CSS-in-JS — styled() / css / keyframes / createGlobalStyle, reactive theming, FNV-1a-deduped StyleSheet (SSR-safe). The `css` tagged template yields a `CSSResult` (resolved on use); `keyframes` stringifies to an animation NAME; `createGlobalStyle` returns a `ComponentFn` that must be MOUNTED. None of them inject CSS at call time — only on resolution/mount.
- @pyreon/unistyle — Responsive breakpoints, CSS property mappings, unit utilities, theme enrichment. Every visual package (`elements`, `rocketstyle`, `coolgrid`, `kinetic`) reads breakpoints / spacing / unit conventions from this package. Override defaults via `enrichTheme()` once at the app root rather than per-component.
- @pyreon/hooks — 41 signal-based hooks: state (useToggle/useCounter/usePrevious/useLatest/useControllableState), DOM (useEventListener/useClickOutside/useFocus/useHover/useFocusTrap/useFocusReturn/useElementSize/useWindowResize/useWindowScroll/useScrollLock/useIntersection/useInfiniteScroll), responsive (useBreakpoint/useMediaQuery/useColorScheme/useReducedMotion/useThemeValue/useSpacing/useRootSize), timing (useDebouncedValue/useDebouncedCallback/useThrottledCallback/useInterval/useTimeout/useTimeAgo), interaction (useClipboard/useHaptics/useDialog/useKeyboard/useOnline/useDocumentVisibility/useIdle), data (useFetch), composition (useMergedRef/useUpdateEffect/useIsomorphicLayoutEffect). `useControllableState({ value, defaultValue, onChange })` is the canonical controlled/uncontrolled pattern. Every primitive in `@pyreon/ui-primitives` uses it. Reimplementing the `isControlled + signal + getter` shape by hand was the #1 anti-pattern across primitives before the helper landed. Pass `value` as a FUNCTION (`() => props.checked`) so the controlled prop read tracks reactively; `defaultValue` is a PLAIN value captured once as the uncontrolled initial.
- @pyreon/elements — Five foundational layout primitives — Element, Text, List, Overlay, Portal — plus the useOverlay positioning hook and the Iterator data helper. Element/Text/List layout props (`direction`, `alignX`, `alignY`, `gap`, `block`, `tag`) are primitive ATTRS — they target the inner flex wrapper. Colors / spacing / borders / shadows belong in the styler or rocketstyle `.theme()` layer. `direction` accepts `inline` / `rows` / `reverseInline` / `reverseRows` — `row` is invalid.
- @pyreon/attrs — Chainable HOC factory — default props (.attrs), base swaps (.config), HOC composition (.compose), statics (.statics). 'priority' does NOT mean 'highest precedence' — priorityAttrs resolve FIRST (so later .attrs() callbacks can read them as input) but sit at the LOWEST precedence in the final merge: priorityAttrs < attrs < explicit props. @pyreon/rocketstyle uses the slot to seed structural base props the dimension layer can still override.
- @pyreon/rocketstyle — Multi-dimensional component styling — states, sizes, variants, custom dimensions, dark/light mode, all cached. useBooleans defaults to FALSE — dimension props take strings (state="primary"), not boolean shorthands. Historically the TYPE default said true while the runtime was false, so boolean props typechecked but were silently dropped; the runtime default (false) is now authoritative in both.
- @pyreon/coolgrid — Context-cascading responsive grid — Container, Row, Col with custom columns and breakpoints. Every numeric grid prop (size/gap/gutter/padding/columns) is responsive — a single number, a mobile-first array ([12, 6, 4]), or a breakpoint-keyed object ({ xs: 12, md: 6 }); size 0 hides the column at that breakpoint.
- @pyreon/kinetic — CSS-transition animations — kinetic(tag) chainable factory, 4 modes (transition/collapse/stagger/group), SSR-safe (peer: @pyreon/core, @pyreon/reactivity, @pyreon/runtime-dom). A transition whose `show` is false at server render still emits its children with the hidden-state class/style inlined (`leaveTo` if defined, else `enterFrom`) — content is structural, animation is visual (Framer Motion / react-transition-group norm). Load-bearing for SSG scroll-reveal where IntersectionObserver can't fire server-side. Trade-off: an INITIALLY-HIDDEN transition with `unmount: true` stays in the DOM (leave-to class applied) after a later leave; initially-visible transitions keep the true-unmount semantic.
- @pyreon/kinetic-presets — 122 animation presets + 5 configurable factories + 5 composition utilities for @pyreon/kinetic. Tree-shaking is first-class: an internal #__NO_SIDE_EFFECTS__ annotation on the preset factory plus sideEffects: false means importing one named preset ships ~300 bytes — importing the `presets` map pins all 122.
- @pyreon/connector-document — Bridge between ui-system JSX trees and @pyreon/document — extracts a DocNode tree for multi-format export. Extraction is a snapshot — reactive accessor children and function-valued `_documentProps` are resolved (called) at extraction time, not subscribed; re-run `extractDocumentTree` after a signal change to export the live state.
- @pyreon/document-primitives — 18 rocketstyle document components — render in browser AND export to 14+ formats via the same tree. `DocDocument` `title` / `author` / `subject` accept either strings or `() => string` accessors. Function values are stored in `_documentProps` and resolved by `extractDocumentTree` at extraction time, so each export click reads the LIVE value from any underlying signal — no `const initial = get()` workaround needed.

```typescript
// PyreonUI — unified provider (replaces 3 separate providers)
import { PyreonUI, useMode } from "@pyreon/ui-core"
import { enrichTheme } from "@pyreon/unistyle"

const theme = enrichTheme({ colors: { primary: "#3b82f6" } })

const App = () => (
  <PyreonUI theme={theme} mode="system">
    <MyApp />
  </PyreonUI>
)

// Read resolved mode anywhere:
const mode = useMode()  // "light" | "dark" (reactive signal)
```
- @pyreon/attrs — placeholder
- @pyreon/rocketstyle — placeholder
- @pyreon/coolgrid — placeholder
- @pyreon/kinetic — placeholder
- @pyreon/kinetic-presets — placeholder
- @pyreon/connector-document — placeholder

## Ecosystem Packages (21)

- @pyreon/store — Global state management — Pinia-inspired composition stores returning StoreApi<T>. Stores are singletons by ID — the setup function runs once. Calling the returned hook multiple times returns the same StoreApi instance. Use `resetStore(id)` or `resetAllStores()` to force re-creation.
- @pyreon/state-tree — Structured reactive state tree — composable models with snapshots, patches, and middleware. State mutations must go through actions — direct `.set()` calls on state signals bypass middleware and patch recording. The model enforces this in dev mode.
- @pyreon/form — Signal-based form management with fields, validation, arrays, and cross-field watchers. Fields validate on blur by default so users aren't scolded mid-keystroke. Use `validateOn: "change"` for instant feedback (often paired with `debounceMs: 300` to avoid thrashing async validators), or `validateOn: "submit"` for zero-feedback-until-submit forms. `showError` (from `useField`) gates on `touched`, so even with `validateOn: "change"` errors won't appear until the user has blurred at least once — this is intentional.
- @pyreon/validation — The library-agnostic validation gate — Standard Schema bridge + Zod / Valibot / ArkType adapters. All three schema libraries are optional peer dependencies. Install only the one you use — the adapters are tree-shaken per import path (`@pyreon/validation/zod`, `@pyreon/validation/valibot`, `@pyreon/validation/arktype`).
- @pyreon/validate — Pyreon's own validation library — chainable + function-comp hybrid API, Standard Schema-native, with built-in field metadata, reactive parse, and i18n-aware error formatting. The protocol deliberately omits a metadata channel — that's the gap `withField` fills. The protocol also doesn't carry i18n keys — `formatErrors` adds that layer.
- @pyreon/query — TanStack Query adapter with signal-driven results + WebSocket subscriptions + SSE (useSSE). `useQuery` / `useInfiniteQuery` / `useQueries` / `useSuspenseQuery` take options as a FUNCTION (not an object) so `queryKey` and other fields can read Pyreon signals. TanStack core uses an object; Pyreon wraps so changing a tracked signal re-runs the observer options and refetches automatically.
- @pyreon/table — Pyreon adapter for TanStack Table — reactive options, signal-driven state, flexRender. Options must be a FUNCTION `() => TableOptions<T>`, not a plain object. Signal reads inside the function are tracked reactively — changing any tracked signal re-syncs the table automatically.
- @pyreon/virtual — Pyreon adapter for TanStack Virtual — element-scoped and window-scoped virtualization. Both hooks return reactive signals (`virtualItems()`, `totalSize()`, `isScrolling()`). Always read them inside reactive scopes (JSX thunks, effect, computed) so they update on scroll.
- @pyreon/i18n — Reactive i18n with Intl number/date/relative-time formatting, plurals, interpolation, async namespaces. `@pyreon/i18n` includes JSX components (Trans, I18nProvider, useI18n) and depends on `@pyreon/core`. `@pyreon/i18n/core` is framework-agnostic with only `@pyreon/reactivity` as a dependency — use it for backends and edge workers.
- @pyreon/feature — Schema-driven CRUD primitives — define once, get queries, forms, tables, and stores. defineFeature composes 5 packages internally (@pyreon/query, @pyreon/form, @pyreon/validation, @pyreon/store, @pyreon/table). All must be installed, and a QueryClient provider must be mounted in the component tree for the query hooks to work.
- @pyreon/a11y — Accessibility primitives — announce() screen-reader live regions, <VisuallyHidden>, createA11yId, zero setup
- @pyreon/charts — Reactive ECharts bridge with lazy loading, auto-detection, typed options. ECharts imports `tslib` whose ESM `./modules/index.js` entry destructures named helpers from a `__toESM(require_tslib())` default — the helpers live as top-level vars on the CJS factory, so the destructure reads `undefined` and the page throws `TypeError: Cannot destructure property "__extends"` the moment ECharts loads. Use `chartsViteAlias()` from `@pyreon/charts/vite` in your `vite.config.ts` (`resolve: { alias: { ...chartsViteAlias() } }`); it resolves `tslib` to the flat-ESM `tslib.es6.js` across install layouts. Browser tests use `tslibBrowserAlias()` from the shared test config. Tracking upstream: microsoft/tslib#189.
- @pyreon/storage — Reactive client-side storage — localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies, IndexedDB. Browser-backed hooks (`useStorage`, `useSessionStorage`, `useIndexedDB`) return the default value on the server. `useCookie` is SSR-readable via `setCookieSource()` which reads from the request headers.
- @pyreon/sync — Local-first CRDT-backed sync for signals — a synced signal IS a signal, so a remote op drives ONE fine-grained DOM update, not a re-render. `@pyreon/sync` (core bridge — engine-free, universal), `@pyreon/sync/yjs` (real Yjs engine + transports + persistence + collaborative text/lists — pulls in `yjs`), `@pyreon/sync/server` (Node/Bun relay — pulls in `ws` + `node:http`, never import into client code).
- @pyreon/hotkeys — Keyboard shortcut management — scope-aware, modifier keys, conflict detection. By default, shortcuts do NOT fire when focused on form elements (input, textarea, select). Pass `enableOnInputs: true` in options to override. Escape is a common candidate for this override.
- @pyreon/permissions — Reactive permissions — RBAC, ABAC, feature flags, subscription tiers. `admin.*` matches exactly one segment (`admin.users`, not `admin.users.list`); use `admin.**` to match any depth below `admin`, and `*` for everything. Resolution is most-specific-first, so an exact or `**` deny overrides a broader subtree grant.
- @pyreon/machine — Reactive state machines — constrained signals with type-safe transitions. Unlike XState, Pyreon machines have no built-in context. Use regular signals alongside the machine for associated data — the machine handles the state transitions, signals handle the data.
- @pyreon/flow — Reactive flow diagrams — signal-native nodes, edges, pan/zoom, auto-layout via elkjs (peer: @pyreon/runtime-dom). LayoutOptions.direction / layerSpacing / edgeRouting apply to layered/tree only — force/stress/radial/box/rectpacking silently ignore them. nodeSpacing is the only field respected by every algorithm. Dev-mode console.warn fires when an option is set on an algorithm that ignores it.
- @pyreon/code — Reactive code editor — CodeMirror 6 with signals, minimap, diff editor, lazy-loaded languages (peer: @pyreon/runtime-dom). `@pyreon/runtime-dom` is required in consumer apps because `<CodeEditor>` JSX emits `_tpl()` / `_bind()` calls.
- @pyreon/rich-text — Reactive WYSIWYG editor — signal-backed layer over TipTap (ProseMirror), lazy-loaded, a11y-labeled (peer: @pyreon/runtime-dom). `@pyreon/runtime-dom` is required in consumer apps because `<RichText>` JSX emits `_tpl()` / `_bind()` calls.
- @pyreon/document — Universal document rendering — 18 primitives, 14+ output formats. Heavy format renderers are lazy-loaded: PDF (~3MB via pdfmake + bundled fonts), DOCX (~700KB via docx), XLSX (~1.1MB via exceljs), PPTX (~400KB via pptxgenjs). First render of each format triggers the dynamic import; subsequent renders are instant. The vendored architecture means apps download all renderer chunks during npm install (14MB total `lib/`), but consumer-side bundlers tree-shake to only ship the renderers an app actually invokes.
- @pyreon/rx — Signal-aware reactive transforms — filter, map, sortBy, groupBy, pipe, debounce, throttle, 37 functions. Detection is purely `typeof source === "function"` (see `isSignal` in `rx/src/types.ts`) — there is NO `.subscribe` / value inspection. Any function (the signal, an accessor wrapper `() => items()`, a bound method) is treated as reactive and called inside a computed. The actual mistake is the opposite of what you might expect: passing a RESOLVED value (`items()`, an already-read array) takes the static path and never updates. Pass the signal, not its resolved value.
- @pyreon/toast — Toast notifications — toast(), toast.success/error/warning/info/loading, Toaster component, a11y (peer: @pyreon/runtime-dom). Unlike most Pyreon packages, toast uses a module-level signal store — `toast()` works from event handlers, effects, or any non-component code. The `<Toaster />` component reads from this shared store.
- @pyreon/url-state — URL-synced state — useUrlState(key, default) or schema mode, auto type coercion, SSR-safe. Type coercion is based on the default value: `useUrlState("page", 1)` coerces `?page=2` to number `2`. `useUrlState("page", "1")` keeps it as string `"1"`. Always provide the right type as default.
- @pyreon/storybook — Storybook renderer for Pyreon components
- @pyreon/mcp — Model Context Protocol server — discoverability map, live API lookup, validation, migration, anti-pattern catalog, changelog, test-environment audit. Each `createServer()` instance maintains its own cached context (routes, components, islands). The cache auto-resets when `process.cwd()` changes between tool invocations, so the same server can operate across multiple projects in one session.
- @pyreon/lint — Pyreon-specific linter — 94 rules across 18 categories, config files, watch mode, AST cache, CLI + LSP
- @pyreon/dnd — Signal-driven drag and drop over @atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop — draggable, droppable, sortable, file drop, monitor. The element-bound hooks (`useDraggable` / `useDroppable` / `useFileDrop`) defer pdnd registration to the next microtask so `ref` callbacks are populated first — call the hook in the component body and let the ref land. `useDragMonitor` registers immediately (it has no element to wait for).

## Zero (Full-Stack Framework)

- @pyreon/zero — Full-stack meta-framework: fs-routing, SSR/SSG/ISR/SPA, API routes, server actions, adapters, i18n. mode: 'ssg' returns Plugin[] (the SSG plugin auto-attaches a companion `ssgPlugin()`); Vite's plugins array flattens nested arrays so `plugins: [pyreon(), zero()]` works as-is.
- @pyreon/zero-content — Compile-time .md/.mdx → Pyreon JSX, typed content collections, convention-scanned MDX components

@pyreon/zero — full-stack meta-framework built on Pyreon + Vite:
- Client-safe main entry: `@pyreon/zero` = client components only, `@pyreon/zero/server` = server-only
- File-system routing (src/routes/) with layouts, error boundaries, loading states, 404 pages
- SSR + SSG + ISR rendering modes — `mode: "ssg"` auto-wires the build-time prerender pipeline (`closeBundle` → SSR sub-build → write `dist/<path>/index.html` per `ssg.paths` entry; auto-detects static paths from the route tree when paths are omitted)
- API routes (src/routes/api/) with typed handlers
- Image optimization — responsive srcset, blur placeholders, WebP/AVIF, lazy loading
- Favicon generation — single source SVG/PNG → ICO, Apple touch, manifest, dark mode SVG
- Locale-aware favicons — `faviconPlugin({ locales: { de: { source: "./icon-de.svg" } } })`
- OG image generation — `ogImagePlugin({ templates, locales })`
- Reactive favicon switching — dual light/dark PNG/ICO with theme-synced media attribute swap
- Meta component — declarative OG (og:image:width/height, og:video, og:audio), noIndex, ogTemplate, favicon prop
- I18n routing — locale detection (Accept-Language + cookie), prefix strategies, useLocale()
- Font optimization — Google Fonts, local fonts, font-display, fallback metrics
- Theme — dark/light toggle with FOUC prevention script
- Middleware composition — compose(), shared context, CORS, cache, compression, rate limiting
- CSP middleware — `cspMiddleware({ directives })` with `useNonce()` for inline scripts
- Env validation — `validateEnv({ PORT: 3000, DEBUG: false, API_KEY: String })`, `publicEnv()`
- Request logging — `loggerMiddleware()`
- AI integration — `aiPlugin()` for llms.txt, JSON-LD inference, AI plugin manifest
- `useRequestLocals()` — bridge middleware locals into component tree
- SEO — sitemap.xml, robots.txt, JSON-LD helpers
- Dev error overlay with stack traces
- Adapters — Node, Bun, static, Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, Netlify Functions
- Layouts receive the route content as `props.children` (zero's `createApp` passes `<RouterView />` as the layout's child); render `{props.children}` inside the layout — do NOT render `<RouterView />` directly, or you'll mount it twice

## Key Ecosystem Patterns

```typescript
// State management
import { defineStore } from '@pyreon/store'
const useCounter = defineStore('counter', () => {
  const count = signal(0)
  const increment = () => count.update(n => n + 1)
  return { count, increment }
})

// Forms
import { useForm, useField } from '@pyreon/form'
const form = useForm({ initialValues: { name: '' }, onSubmit: (v) => save(v) })
const name = useField(form, 'name')

// Permissions
import { createPermissions } from '@pyreon/permissions'
const can = createPermissions({ 'posts.read': true, 'posts.delete': (post) => post.authorId === userId })

// State machines
import { createMachine } from '@pyreon/machine'
const light = createMachine({ initial: 'red', states: { red: { on: { NEXT: 'green' } }, green: { on: { NEXT: 'yellow' } }, yellow: { on: { NEXT: 'red' } } } })

// Document rendering
import { createDocument } from '@pyreon/document'
const doc = createDocument({ title: 'Report' }).heading('Sales').table({ columns: ['Region', 'Revenue'], rows: [['US', '$1M']] })
await doc.toPdf()   // PDF
await doc.toEmail() // Outlook-safe HTML
await doc.toSlack() // Slack Block Kit JSON

// Reactive transforms
import { filter, pipe, sortBy } from '@pyreon/rx'
const items = signal([{ name: 'B', age: 30 }, { name: 'A', age: 25 }])
const adults = filter(items, i => i.age >= 18)  // Computed<Item[]>
const sorted = pipe(items, sortBy('name'), filter(i => i.age > 20))

// Toast notifications
import { toast, Toaster } from '@pyreon/toast'
toast.success('Saved!')
toast.promise(saveData(), { loading: 'Saving...', success: 'Done!', error: 'Failed' })

// URL-synced state
import { useUrlState } from '@pyreon/url-state'
const page = useUrlState('page', 1)     // synced to ?page=
const filters = useUrlState({ page: 1, sort: 'name' })  // schema mode

// SSE subscription
import { useSSE } from '@pyreon/query'
const { data } = useSSE({ queryKey: ['events'], url: '/api/events' })

// Active route detection
import { useIsActive } from '@pyreon/router'
const isAdmin = useIsActive('/admin')  // reactive boolean

// Typed search params with auto-coercion
import { useTypedSearchParams } from '@pyreon/router'
const params = useTypedSearchParams({ page: 'number', q: 'string' })
params.page()  // number

// Route transition state — useTransition() returns a reactive accessor () => boolean
import { useTransition } from '@pyreon/router'
const isTransitioning = useTransition()  // call it: isTransitioning()

// View Transitions API — auto-enabled, opt out per route:
{ path: '/modal', component: Modal, meta: { viewTransition: false } }
// `await router.push()` / `.replace()` resolves AFTER the DOM swap (vt.updateCallbackDone),
// NOT after the full animation (.finished, 200-300ms). afterEach hooks + scrollManager.restore
// also fire post-commit — they see the NEW route state.

// Middleware chain with data passing
const auth: RouteMiddleware = async (ctx) => { ctx.data.user = await getUser(ctx.request) }
// In component: const data = useMiddlewareData()  // accessor → read via data().user
```

## Documentation

Full docs at `docs/` (Pyreon-native site, 95 pages): `cd docs && bun run dev`

## AI Tools

- `pyreon doctor --json` — scan project for React patterns, get structured fix suggestions
- `pyreon doctor --fix` — auto-fix React patterns to Pyreon equivalents
- `pyreon context` — generate .pyreon/context.json with routes, components, islands
- MCP server: `bunx @pyreon/mcp` — live API lookup, validation, migration, diagnostics

## Dev-mode warnings — bundler tree-shake

Pyreon dev warnings are gated on `import.meta.env?.DEV`. Source pattern × bundler tree-shake:

- inline `if (!import.meta.env?.DEV) return` — tree-shaken in both Vite and raw esbuild.
- `const __DEV__ = ...; if (__DEV__) ...` — tree-shaken in Vite, mostly tree-shaken in raw esbuild.
- `const __DEV__ = ...; __DEV__ && cond && warn(...)` (chained &&) — tree-shaken in Vite, runtime-gated only in raw esbuild (string survives in bundle but warn doesn't fire because gate is false).
- `typeof process !== 'undefined'` — dead code in all browser bundlers; forbidden by `pyreon/no-process-dev-gate` lint rule.

Vite is Pyreon's primary supported bundler. Non-Vite consumers using the chained form pay a small bundle-size cost only — runtime is correct. Tests live in `runtime-dom/src/tests/dev-gate-treeshake.test.ts` (Vite + non-Vite runtime smoke) and `flow/src/tests/integration.test.ts` (esbuild for the inline pattern).
